description_long: This sculpture depicts a mummiform figure reminiscent of ancient Egyptian funerary art, standing upright on a rectangular plinth. The figure is wrapped in a textured shroud, with detailed facial features and a headdress that frames the face. The arms are crossed over the chest, a pose typical of Egyptian sarcophagi, and the hands are visible, holding small objects. The surface coloration transitions from a darker brown at the back to a lighter, almost sandy hue at the front, suggesting weathering or intentional patination. The plinth is a solid, dark base that provides stability and contrast to the figure above. The overall form is slender and elongated, with subtle incised patterns along the body to suggest wrappings or inscriptions.
description: A slender, mummiform sculpture with detailed facial features and crossed arms, standing on a dark rectangular plinth. The coloration shifts from dark brown to sandy beige, evoking ancient Egyptian sarcophagi.
description_view_0: Side view showing the elongated profile, the headdress, and the textured wrappings along the body.
description_view_1: Frontal view highlighting the detailed face, crossed arms, and the lighter coloration of the front surface.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, revealing the headdress and the transition from light to dark coloration along the body.
description_view_3: Rear view displaying the back of the headdress, the vertical lines of the wrappings, and the solid plinth.
attribute_subject_matter: mummiform human figure in Egyptian style
attribute_location_context: No specific setting is visible; the object appears isolated against a plain background.
